Class AtomicDoubleArray

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicDoubleArray
All Implemented Interfaces:
it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.doubles.DoubleIterable, Serializable, Iterable<Double>
public class AtomicDoubleArray extends Object implements it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.doubles.DoubleIterable, Serializable
A double array in which elements can be updated atomically. Internally stores a long[]. Can also be used in enhanced for (for-each) loops, but it's recommended to use DoubleIterable.forEach(DoubleConsumer) to avoid (un)boxing.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    AtomicDoubleArray(double[] array)
    Creates a new AtomicDoubleArray with the same length as, and all elements copied from, the given array.
    AtomicDoubleArray(int length)
    Creates a new AtomicDoubleArray of the given length, with all elements initially zero.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    final double
    addAndGet(int i, double delta)
    Atomically adds the given value to the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
    final boolean
    compareAndSet(int i, double expectedValue, double newValue)
    Atomically sets the element at index i to newValue if the element's current value == expectedValue, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
    final double
    decrementAndGet(int i)
    Atomically decrements the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
    final double
    get(int i)
    Returns the current value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.getVolatile(Object...).
    final double
    getAndAdd(int i, double delta)
    Atomically adds the given value to the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
    final double
    getAndDecrement(int i)
    Atomically decrements the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
    final double
    getAndIncrement(int i)
    Atomically increments the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
    final double
    getAndSet(int i, double newValue)
    Atomically sets the element at index i to newValue and returns the old value, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.getAndSet(Object...).
    final long
    getAndUpdate(int i, DoubleUnaryOperator updateFunction)
    Atomically updates (with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...)) the element at index i with the results of applying the given function, returning the previous value.
    final double
    incrementAndGet(int i)
    Atomically increments the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
    it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.doubles.DoubleIterator
    iterator()
    final int
    length()
    Returns the length of the array.
    final void
    set(int i, double newValue)
    Sets the element at index i to newValue, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.setVolatile(Object...).
    final long
    updateAndGet(int i, DoubleUnaryOperator updateFunction)
    Atomically updates (with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...)) the element at index i with the results of applying the given function, returning the updated value.

  • Constructor Details

    • AtomicDoubleArray

      public AtomicDoubleArray(int length)
      Creates a new AtomicDoubleArray of the given length, with all elements initially zero.
      Parameters:
      length - the length of the array

    • AtomicDoubleArray

      public AtomicDoubleArray(double[] array)
      Creates a new AtomicDoubleArray with the same length as, and all elements copied from, the given array.
      Parameters:
      array - the array to copy elements from
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if array is null

  • Method Details

    • length

      public final int length()
      Returns the length of the array.
      Returns:
      the length of the array

    • get

      public final double get(int i)
      Returns the current value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.getVolatile(Object...).
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      Returns:
      the current value

    • set

      public final void set(int i, double newValue)
      Sets the element at index i to newValue, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.setVolatile(Object...).
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      newValue - the new value

    • getAndSet

      public final double getAndSet(int i, double newValue)
      Atomically sets the element at index i to newValue and returns the old value, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.getAndSet(Object...).
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      newValue - the new value
      Returns:
      the previous value

    • compareAndSet

      public final boolean compareAndSet(int i, double expectedValue, double newValue)
      Atomically sets the element at index i to newValue if the element's current value == expectedValue, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      expectedValue - the expected value
      newValue - the new value
      Returns:
      true if successful. False return indicates that the actual value was not equal to the expected value.

    • getAndIncrement

      public final double getAndIncrement(int i)
      Atomically increments the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).

      Equivalent to getAndAdd(i, 1.0).

      Parameters:
      i - the index
      Returns:
      the previous value

    • getAndDecrement

      public final double getAndDecrement(int i)
      Atomically decrements the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).

      Equivalent to getAndAdd(i, -1.0).

      Parameters:
      i - the index
      Returns:
      the previous value

    • getAndAdd

      public final double getAndAdd(int i, double delta)
      Atomically adds the given value to the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      delta - the value to add
      Returns:
      the previous value

    • incrementAndGet

      public final double incrementAndGet(int i)
      Atomically increments the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).

      Equivalent to getAndAdd(i, 1.0).

      Parameters:
      i - the index
      Returns:
      the updated value

    • decrementAndGet

      public final double decrementAndGet(int i)
      Atomically decrements the value of the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).

      Equivalent to getAndAdd(i, -1.0).

      Parameters:
      i - the index
      Returns:
      the updated value

    • addAndGet

      public final double addAndGet(int i, double delta)
      Atomically adds the given value to the element at index i, with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...).
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      delta - the value to add
      Returns:
      the updated value

    • getAndUpdate

      public final long getAndUpdate(int i, DoubleUnaryOperator updateFunction)
      Atomically updates (with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...)) the element at index i with the results of applying the given function, returning the previous value. The function should be side-effect-free, since it may be re-applied when attempted updates fail due to contention among threads.
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      updateFunction - a side-effect-free function
      Returns:
      the previous value

    • updateAndGet

      public final long updateAndGet(int i, DoubleUnaryOperator updateFunction)
      Atomically updates (with memory effects as specified by VarHandle.compareAndSet(Object...)) the element at index i with the results of applying the given function, returning the updated value. The function should be side-effect-free, since it may be re-applied when attempted updates fail due to contention among threads.
      Parameters:
      i - the index
      updateFunction - a side-effect-free function
      Returns:
      the updated value

    • iterator

      public it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.doubles.DoubleIterator iterator()
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.doubles.DoubleIterable
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<Double>
      Returns:
